note: Stub — future agent will update resourceIds and prompts nightly from lesson progress

modes.label: 10 min
modes.description: Active recall sprint — 1 concept, audio-friendly, ideal for short breaks
reviewPrompts: In one sentence: what is linear algebra actually used for in ML?
What is the geometric meaning of a dot product?
Why do neural networks rely on matrix multiplication?

modes.label: 20 min
modes.description: Standard review — 2 topics, covers main concepts from current and prior week
reviewPrompts: What is the difference between variance and standard deviation?
Explain conditional probability with a real example.
How does a normal distribution arise from the Central Limit Theorem?

modes.label: 30 min
modes.description: Deep review — 3 resources, exercises and active recall across two weeks
reviewPrompts: Walk through Gaussian elimination step-by-step in your head.
What is Bayes' theorem and when would you apply it in ML?
How does PCA use eigenvectors to reduce dimensions?

modes.label: Walking
modes.description: Audio-only — no screen needed, verbal recall, one concept per block
reviewPrompts: Explain vectors out loud as if talking to a friend who's never studied math.
What does it mean for a matrix transformation to be 'linear'?
Give one example where probability helps a machine learn something.

modes.label: Gym
modes.description: High-energy mode — broad inspiring ideas, big-picture applications of ML math
reviewPrompts: What would you need to build a recommendation system from scratch?
Why do all AI models ultimately reduce to matrix math?
What ML application genuinely excites you — and what math does it require?

modes.label: Passive
modes.description: Background listening — cooking, cleaning, grooming. Let the audio play.
reviewPrompts: Let the audio play — when you hear an unfamiliar term, note it for later.
Catch any key concept and try to repeat it back in your head.
No pressure — passive exposure compounds over time.
